Container.Name: garage
Container.Overview: Garage: S3-compatible distributed object storage, used here as the backing store for Milvus (replacing MinIO). Before first install: create the shared bridge network with `docker network create milvus-net` on the Unraid host. Also create `/mnt/cache/appdata/garage/{meta,data}` and drop a `garage.toml` at `/mnt/cache/appdata/garage/garage.toml` (starter in repo under config-examples/). After first start: `docker exec garage /garage status` to confirm healthy, then run the bootstrap script to create the Milvus bucket + access key (see repo README).
Container.Description: Garage is a lightweight, efficient, S3-compatible object store designed for geo-distributed deployment. On Unraid it runs as a single-node replacement for MinIO and backs the Milvus vector database.

Config.Name: S3 API
Config.Description: S3-compatible HTTP API (the port Milvus talks to). Expose to LAN only if you want external S3 clients.

Config.Name: Admin API
Config.Description: Admin HTTP API — health checks and metrics. Exposing to LAN is optional.

Config.Name: RPC
Config.Description: Cluster RPC port. Not needed for single-node deploys; leave unset.

Config.Name: Metadata
Config.Description: Metadata dir (LMDB/sqlite). MUST live on SSD / cache pool — never on /mnt/user.

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Bulk data dir. Can live on the array for cheap storage; cache pool is fine too.

Config.Name: Config File
Config.Description: Path on host to garage.toml. See config-examples/garage.toml in the template repo for a starter.
